message:
refactor(database/postgres): use FrontendMessage enum for wire bytes and remove dead code

Replace raw byte literals (b'p', b'Q', b'X') with the existing
FrontendMessage enum variants (Password, Query, Terminate) throughout
the PostgreSQL protocol implementation. This eliminates the gap between
the enum definition and its usage, making the protocol layer self-
documenting and reducing the risk of typo-induced protocol violations.

Also:
- Remove unused FrontendMessage::Flush variant (was never constructed)
- Remove unused ScramAuth::username field (the username is already
  baked into client_first_bare during construction and never read again)
- Gate BackendMessage enum, MessageBuffer::clear(), and
  MessageBuffer::into_inner() with #[cfg(test)] since they are only
  used in test code, reducing the surface of the non-test binary

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
